title: Please Set Me Free
framing: Consent-first civic reflection poll. Every answer is a policy sentiment / lived-experience signal — never a diagnosis, never a label.

consent.intro: This interactive song poll asks optional questions about policy, community, identity, and lived experience. Your answers help build aggregate civic research for Equity Uprise. You can skip any question.

choices.label: I agree to save my poll answers for aggregate research.
I agree to answer optional demographic questions.
I agree to be contacted about policy/fellowship/civic work.

consent.after_answer: Your answer is saved as an issue-signal, not a diagnosis.

markers.label: Freedom Pressure
markers.definition: A felt demand for relief from systems, rules, costs, or conditions that limit life choices.

markers.label: Policy Burden
markers.definition: The feeling that laws, rules, or public systems make everyday life harder.

markers.label: Corporate Extraction
markers.definition: The perception that companies profit from communities without giving fair value back.

markers.label: Civic Distrust
markers.definition: Low confidence that government or elected officials care, listen, or act fairly.

markers.label: Creative Resistance
markers.definition: Turning frustration into writing, music, organizing, or public testimony.

markers.label: Place Attachment
markers.definition: The emotional and identity connection people have to a city, neighborhood, or home.

markers.label: Capital Exclusion
markers.definition: Barriers to loans, grants, credit, or investment.

markers.label: Displacement Pressure
markers.definition: The pressure people feel when redevelopment, rents, taxes, or costs threaten their ability to stay.

markers.label: Utility Siting Concern
markers.definition: Concern about power lines, monopoles, energy projects, infrastructure siting, and community consent.

markers.label: Criminal-Legal Trap
markers.definition: The feeling that laws, probation, fees, or enforcement cycles keep people under control instead of helping them rebuild.

markers.label: Political Stress
markers.definition: Emotional strain connected to public rhetoric, elections, policy, or government action.

markers.label: Immigration Fear
markers.definition: Fear, disruption, or family stress connected to immigration enforcement or anti-immigration rhetoric.

markers.label: Civic Efficacy
markers.definition: Belief that action — voting, protest, art, organizing, legal work — can actually change conditions.

markers.label: Hope Delay
markers.definition: The feeling that justice may come too late or not within one's lifetime.

modules.name: Freedom pressure, policy burden, corporate greed
Change, hardship, blame, and creative resistance
Lifestyle, utility infrastructure, war language, government trust
Immigration, freedom, hope, no way out

units.bar: Please set me free,
units.meaning: This line is about more than escape. It asks what kind of system, rule, cost, or condition is making life feel restricted.
units.question: When you hear "set me free," what kind of freedom comes to mind first?

units.bar: and just let me be
units.meaning: The ask for autonomy — room to live without interference.
units.question: Do you feel institutions give people like you enough room to live without interference?

units.bar: Cause all these policies and all this corporate greed
units.meaning: Government rules and private-sector extraction, named in one breath.
units.question: Which feels heavier in your life right now?

units.bar: It really gets to me
units.meaning: The emotional weight of public conditions.
units.question: How much do public issues affect your mood or stress level?

units.bar: Please set me free… (again)
units.meaning: Second exposure to the hook — how does it land now?
units.question: After hearing it again, does the line feel personal, political, spiritual, or artistic?

units.bar: …policies and corporate greed (again)
units.meaning: Which arena does this song live in for you?
units.question: Which policy area do you connect to this most?

units.bar: It really gets to me, (again)
units.meaning: Intensity after repetition.
units.question: Would you say these issues affect you rarely, sometimes, often, or constantly?

units.bar: Cause my people in chains
units.meaning: Collective constraint — the community carried in two words.
units.question: Optional: when you hear "my people," what community are you thinking about?

units.bar: We ain't diggin' through the couch but what we seekin' is change
units.meaning: Change as money and change as transformation — same word, same hunger.
units.question: What kind of change would matter most in your community?

units.bar: Cause we done been through hell
units.meaning: Accumulated burden — carried collectively.
units.question: Do you feel your community has been through more than people outside it understand?

units.bar: And I been peepin' the flames
units.meaning: Seeing the crisis before anyone official names it.
units.question: Do you usually notice community problems before officials respond?

units.bar: All these crooked politicians, they the people to blame
units.meaning: Accountability, named directly.
units.question: How much do you trust elected officials to act in your community's interest?

units.bar: So please set me free, and just let me live
units.meaning: Survival frame — not luxury, living.
units.question: Do current policies help you live better, make life harder, or feel invisible?

units.bar: Y'all done took so much, got nothin' left to give
units.meaning: Extraction until empty.
units.question: What feels most drained right now?

units.bar: Wait I take that back, what I got left's a fist
units.meaning: After depletion, resistance remains.
units.question: When you feel pushed too far, what response feels most natural?

units.bar: And I'ma use it to write, so just accept this gift
units.meaning: Anger converted into art, testimony, civic voice.
units.question: Would you use art, writing, music, or media to speak on policy issues?

units.bar: Leave my city alone
units.meaning: Local control. No exact addresses — a city name is enough.
units.question: Optional: what city or area are you answering from?

units.bar: This is BPT, I call this city my home…
units.meaning: Place as identity.
units.question: How connected do you feel to your city or neighborhood?

units.bar: But we ain't gettin' no loans, we gotta get it alone
units.meaning: Credit access, lending exclusion, self-financing.
units.question: Have you or your family ever felt blocked from loans, credit, grants, or business capital?

units.bar: Now you movin' into town, and we ain't gettin' no say
units.meaning: Development without community voice.
units.question: Do residents in your area get enough say before major development happens?

units.bar: With all this gentrification, we ain't gettin' to stay
units.meaning: Displacement risk — rents, taxes, redevelopment.
units.question: Have rising rents, taxes, or redevelopment made it harder for people you know to stay?

units.bar: Y'all been eatin' off of us, and we ain't gettin' a plate
units.meaning: Investment without benefit-sharing.
units.question: When investment comes into your community, who benefits most?

units.bar: …policies… corporate greed… (the neighborhood weight)
units.meaning: Which local fight should this song collect data on first?
units.question: Which local issue should this song collect data on first?

units.bar: I just wanna live life… diamond watch would be nice, but I ain't rockin' with ice
units.meaning: Stability over status.
units.question: Which matters more to you right now?

units.bar: All this talk about these monopoles just isn't right…
units.meaning: The song walks straight into Docket 516 — utility siting and community consent.
units.question: Have utility poles, power lines, energy bills, or infrastructure projects affected your neighborhood?

units.bar: There's a war outside, my people under attack
units.meaning: Perceived threat, collective vulnerability.
units.question: Do you feel your community is under pressure right now?

units.bar: The government don't care about us… y'all was settin' a trap, now we all set in a trap
units.meaning: Where probation, fees, and enforcement cycles live in this song.
units.question: Do criminal laws, probation rules, or marijuana enforcement feel fair, unfair, or like they keep people trapped?

sensitive_followups.question: Optional: which state is your view based on?
Optional: has probation touched your life?

units.bar: I don't wanna be here… I just wanna be clear
units.meaning: Political stress and emotional burden — not a diagnosis, ever.
units.question: Over the last two weeks, how often has political news or public rhetoric left you feeling down, hopeless, or emotionally drained?

units.bar: All this anti-immigration, got my cousins in fear
units.meaning: Family impact from immigration policy and rhetoric.
units.question: Have immigration laws, enforcement, or rhetoric negatively affected you or your family?

units.bar: My fist is up in the air, cause the freedom up in the air
units.meaning: Protest posture, uncertain outcome.
units.question: Do you feel protest, voting, art, or organizing can change anything?

units.bar: Can't wait for the day… who we kiddin', I'll be waitin' till the day that I'm gone
units.meaning: The most valuable question in the song.
units.question: Do you feel like there is a real way out of the issues named in this song?

branch.question: What would need to change first?

units.bar: Please set me free… (final hook, first pass)
units.meaning: Post-exposure re-check: did the song move you?
units.question: After hearing the full verse, which issue feels most urgent now?

units.bar: Please set me free… (final hook, second pass)
units.meaning: Resonance check.
units.question: Did this song make the issue feel more personal, more political, both, or neither?

units.bar: Please set me free… (final hook, third pass)
units.meaning: Action readiness.
units.question: Would you take one action on this issue today?

units.bar: Please set me free… (final hook, fourth pass)
units.meaning: Keep your signal.
units.question: Do you want to save your answers and see your policy profile?

units.bar: So please set me free
units.meaning: The closing word is yours.
units.question: In one word, what should people be set free from?

demographics.note: Optional, multi-select, self-described — modeled on the 2024 combined race/ethnicity standard. Never required, never sent to GA4.
demographics.question: Optional: how do you describe your race and/or ethnicity? Select all that apply, or describe it your own way.

political_trust.cta: Make them listen — join the outreach work

community_wellbeing.label: community wellbeing
community_wellbeing.cta: The movement takes care of its own — walk with Equity Uprise

creative_civic_action.label: creative civic action
creative_civic_action.cta: Submit a civic anthem — your art belongs in the release

support_card: Heavy is real. Before the politics: breathe, step outside, call somebody who knows your name. If the weight stays, 988 (call or text) is free and always on. The movement needs you whole.
